calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-11-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Ioakimedes for and with Jordan Goodman.

Mr. Ioakimedes stated that there should be a proration per the terms of the will, that the Executor will not require any beneficiary to pay the tax (cites Silveria case), that the proration can affect the charities, and that the Executor will not seek any reimbursement. In addition, Mr. Ioakimedes stated that no one had appeared to object/challenge. The matter was taken under submission. The Court stated that if it did not agree it would give Counsel an additional opportunity to argue.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Reporter: Bob Balian, CSR#. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-02-2009 Continued To Jun-09-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-19-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Ioakimedes for and with Jordan Goodman (Petitioner).

Mr. Ioakimedes stated that the substitution of attorney by Tobin and Tobin was going to be filed today, that Mr. Wilson would not be making an appearance and that Mr. Wilson was withdrawing his objections. The Court granted the petition per written order.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Linda Forster CSR#13286. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-30-2008 Continued To Aug-19-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven Wilson for Ronald Fallai. Lawrence Bertoncini for Jordan Goodman.

This matter was pre-granted. Mr. Wilson appeared to request a continuance stating that he found out about the matter on Friday, that the Executor was the care custodian of the Decedent and that the will which disinherited his client had been executed 15 days prior to the Decedent's death. In addition he stated that notice was mailed to the wrong address and that he had filed two declarations this morning. Mr. Bertoncini stated that he was not in agreement with the continuance, that his client was named in the will, that Mr. Wilson was making unsubstantiated allegations, requested the petition be granted and stated that Mr. Wilson could file a will contest if needed. The Court continued the matter to August 19, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bonner.
